title: Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships 2027: Fully Funded Postgraduate Study in New Zealand
summary: Applications for the Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships will open on March 1, 2026. Funded by the Government of New Zealand , these fully funded scholarships support applicants from eligible countries to undertake full-time postgraduate studies in 2027. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); This prestigious program aims to build leadership capacity and contribute to sustainable development in partner countries. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Scholarship Overview The Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships provide full financial support for selected international students pursuing postgraduate qualifications in New Zealand. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Priority Sectors Applicants must align their proposed studies with one of the following priority development areas: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Climate resilience, science and environment Economic resilience and the private sector Food security and agriculture Governance Infrastructure Peace, security and justice Resilient societies Programs of study should clearly demonstrate how they contribute to development outcomes in the applicant s home country. Eligible Educational Institutions Scholarship recipients may study at the following New Zealand institutions: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Auckland University of Technology Lincoln University Massey University University of Auckland University of Canterbury University of Otago University of Waikato Victoria University of Wellington Southern Institute of Technology Unitec Institute of Technology Applicants must apply to eligible programs offered at these institutions. Who Can Apply? Applicants must: Be from an eligible country Apply for full-time postgraduate study (Master s or PhD level) Demonstrate alignment with priority development sectors Meet academic and English language requirements Intend to return to their home country after completion Key Dates to Remember Applications Open: March 1, 2026 Applications Close: March 30, 2026 ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Late applications are not accepted, so early preparation is strongly advised. Application Period March 1 March 30, 2026 Official Scholarship Information. Eligible Countries List. For more OFY Schorlarship opportunities, click here. #Scholarships #StudyInNewZealand #ManaakiScholarships #FullyFunded #Postgraduate The post Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships 2027: Fully Funded Postgraduate Study in New Zealand appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: 5 Fully Funded Commonwealth Scholarships for MSc in Global Development at The Open University (U.K.)
summary: If you are a Malawian development professional eager to advance your career in global development, peace, security and governance , this is one of the most prestigious and high-impact scholarship opportunities available this year. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); About the Programme MSc in Global Development This Master of Science (MSc) in Global Development focuses on building skills that strengthen: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Peace and security Development policy analysis Governance and leadership in development settings ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); It is delivered via distance learning , meaning you can continue working and contributing to your community while pursuing your studies. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The course is taught by The Open University and delivered in partnership with Civil Society Agriculture Network (CISANET) in Malawi. What s on Offer? The Commonwealth Distance Learning Scholarships have awarded five fully funded awards worth 90,000 to support Malawian nationals to study for the highly respected MSc in Global Development delivered by The Open University (U.K.) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Why This Scholarship Matters Fully funded scholarships (tuition covered) Study a globally recognised Master s degree from a top UK distance-learning institution Continue working at home while studying part-time Become a leader in development policy, peace, governance and security Make a measurable impact in your community and country This programme has supported over 220 East African professionals to date. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Benefits Here s what the scholarship includes: 1. Full tuition fee coverage for the MSc 2. Ability to study part-time via distance learning 3. A Master s degree from a respected UK university 4. Enhanced skills in development policy, governance, peace and security 5. Boosted professional credibility and career prospects Note : Scholarships generally cover tuition only; living costs are your responsibility. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Eligibility Criteria To be considered for one of the five Commonwealth Scholarships, applicants must: 1. Be Malawian nationals 2. Be employed by a development agency (government, NGO or private sector) in Malawi 3. Have at least two years of professional experience working on development issues in Malawi 4. Hold a good undergraduate degree (preferably in a relevant development field) 5. Demonstrate a strong commitment to local and national development goals 6. Meet scholarship and degree entry requirements set by the Foreign, Commonwealth Development Office (FCDO) and CSC. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); This scholarship is designed for professionals with leadership potential and practical experience. Application Process To apply: Step 1 Create a CSC Account Go to the Commonwealth Scholarship Commission (CSC) application portal and sign up. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); You will find the eligible course MSc Global Development (distance learning) listed under Group 4: Strengthening global peace, security and governance . Step 2 Complete the Online Application Form Apply here Important: Provide a detailed explanation of how your degree will benefit Malawi . This narrative is a key part of the selection process. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Step 3 Submit Before Deadline Submit all required materials before 16:00 GMT, 31st March 2026 . Important Dates Deadlines Deadline Detail 31st March 2026 16:00 GMT Final deadline to submit the scholarship application online. October 2026 Expected programme start date for MSc in Global Development scholars. Pro Tip: Submit early late or incomplete applications will not be considered. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); This is a rare and highly competitive scholarship for development professionals in Malawi one that can truly elevate your career and multiply your impact back home.
